summary: Ryser's conjecture remains open for all $r \geq 4$. The $r = 3$ case was proved by Aharoni in 2001 (before the problem was posted). Since posting, Haxell and Scott (c. 2012) proved the weaker bound $\tau \leq (r - \varepsilon)\nu$ for $r = 4, 5$; DeBiasio et al. (2021) surveyed known results, improved some, and introduced new stronger variants; and Bishnoi et al. (2020) resolved the $t$-intersecting variant for $r \leq \tfrac{36}{7}t - 5$.

since_posted.title: Generalizations and strengthenings of Ryser's conjecture
since_posted.authors: Louis DeBiasio, Yigal Kamel, Grace McCourt, Hannah Sheats
since_posted.claim: Surveys known results on Ryser's conjecture (open for $r \geq 4$), improves some bounds, and introduces new stronger conjectures and generalizations to multipartite graphs and hypergraphs.

since_posted.title: Ryser's Conjecture for $t$-intersecting hypergraphs
since_posted.authors: Anurag Bishnoi, Shagnik Das, Patrick Morris, Tibor Szabó
since_posted.claim: Proves the $t$-intersecting variant of Ryser's conjecture ($\tau(\mathcal{H}) \leq r - t$) for all $r \leq \tfrac{36}{7}t - 5$, significantly extending the previously known range.

search_queries: Ryser's conjecture r-uniform r-partite hypergraph tau nu progress 2007-2026
Ryser conjecture 4-partite hypergraph cover matching proof arXiv
Ryser conjecture r=4 solved partial results Haxell Aharoni 2010 2020
arXiv Ryser conjecture hypergraph recent progress survey 2015 2020 2025
notes: The most significant post-2007 general result is by Haxell and Scott (c. 2012): $\tau(H) \leq (r - \varepsilon)\nu(H)$ for some $\varepsilon > 0$ when $r \in \{4,5\}$ — but the paper PDF at https://people.maths.ox.ac.uk/scott/Papers/ryser.pdf was not machine-readable, so it could not be included as a verified citation. Tuza's result ($\nu=1$, $r \leq 5$) and Aharoni's $r=3$ proof both predate the posting. The Bishnoi et al. paper also appeared in J. Combin. Theory Ser. A (pii/S0097316520301588) but only the arXiv URL was verified.
